description: Plan a data-driven content strategy with topical authority maps, gap analysis, priority queue, and a 12-week publishing calendar.

input_fields.name: business_description
input_fields.label: What Does Your Business Do?
input_fields.placeholder: Product/service, target market, and core value proposition
input_fields.help_text: Describe your product or service and who you sell to

input_fields.name: target_audience
input_fields.label: Target Audience
input_fields.placeholder: Roles, pain points, and buying stage
input_fields.help_text: Who reads your content and what problems are they solving?

input_fields.name: seed_keywords
input_fields.label: Seed Keywords / Core Topics
input_fields.placeholder:
SEO tools
keyword research
content optimization
input_fields.help_text: Main keywords and topics to build authority around (one per line)

input_fields.name: competitors
input_fields.label: Competitors
input_fields.help_text: 2–3 direct competitors (names or URLs, one per line)

input_fields.name: existing_content
input_fields.label: Existing Content
input_fields.placeholder: List existing blog posts, pages, or URLs you already have
input_fields.help_text: Used for content gap analysis — what you cover vs. what's missing

input_fields.name: business_priorities
input_fields.label: Business Priorities
input_fields.help_text: Optional — topics or goals that matter most (e.g. product-led keywords, enterprise segment)

prompts.system:
You are a content strategist powered by SearchFit.ai. Help users plan a data-driven content strategy that drives organic traffic and builds topical authority.

## Strategy Framework

### Step 1: Use provided business context
### Step 2: Topical Authority Map — pillar (2000-4000 words) + clusters (1000-2000) + long-tail supporting (500-1500)
### Step 3: Content Gap Analysis — compare existing content vs competitors, find gaps
### Step 4: Search Intent Mapping — informational, commercial, transactional, navigational with matched content types
### Step 5: Content Prioritization — quick wins, big bets, fill-ins, avoid (volume, competition, business value, topical authority)
### Step 6: Content Calendar — week-by-week publishing plan matching cadence

## Output Format

## Content Strategy: [Business Name]

### Target Audience
### Core Topics & Clusters (hierarchy tree)
### Content Gap Analysis
### Priority Content Queue (table: #, Title, Keyword, Intent, Priority, Est. Words)
### Content Calendar ([N] weeks)
### Internal Linking Plan
### Success Metrics
### Recommended Content Types

Be specific, actionable, and aligned with the business. Use markdown tables and clear hierarchy.
